stories.title: Texas Softball Returns Home — McCombs Field Championship Celebration Draws Thousands

x_posts.label: Post 1-1
x_posts.text: Texas is home. The national champion Longhorns were welcomed at McCombs Field Friday night — free admission, Bevo, the Longhorn Band, and a city that knows it has the best softball program in America. 🥎 #HookEm #WCWS

x_posts.label: Post 1-2
x_posts.text: Back-to-back national champions. Texas softball finished as part of a three-championship haul for the Longhorns in 2025-26 — men's swimming & diving, rowing, and now softball. Austin is college sports' best city right now. #TexasSoftball

article.headline: Home Is Where the Trophy Is: Texas Brings WCWS Title Back to a City Ready to Celebrate

stories.title: 2026 WCWS All-Tournament Team Announced — Twelve Players, Five Programs

x_posts.label: Post 2-1
x_posts.text: The 2026 WCWS All-Tournament Team is set. Teagan Kavan (MOP), Katie Stewart (Texas), NiJaree Canady, Jocelyn Briski (Alabama), Jordy Frahm (Nebraska). Twelve players. One championship. The best in the game. #WCWS

x_posts.label: Post 2-2
x_posts.text: Alabama sent three players to the WCWS All-Tournament Team: Jocelyn Briski, Marlie Giles, and Jena Young. Texas Tech put four on the list despite losing the finals. Texas had two. Nebraska one. The sport's best tournament gives its best honors. #WCWS

article.headline: Kavan, Canady, Briski, Frahm: The 2026 WCWS All-Tournament Team Honors Twelve Across Five Programs

stories.title: Softball Transfer Portal Opens June 8 — Post-WCWS Roster Movement Begins

x_posts.label: Post 3
x_posts.text: The softball transfer portal opens June 8 — a 15-day window running through June 22. Tennessee's Saviya Morgan and Kailey Plumlee have already announced they're entering. Roster movement is coming for every program in the country. #CollegeSoftball

article.headline: Tennessee Already Losing Players as Softball's Transfer Portal Opens Monday June 8

stories.title: 2026 WCWS Sets New All-Time Attendance Record at Devon Park

x_posts.label: Post 4
x_posts.text: The 2026 Women's College World Series set a new attendance record — over 105,000 fans across the tournament's nine days at Devon Park. The opening session drew a record 12,605. No Oklahoma. No problem. The sport is growing. #WCWS

article.headline: No Oklahoma, No Problem: 2026 WCWS Sets New All-Time Attendance Record at Devon Park

stories.title: Kavan and Stewart Return for 2027 — The Three-Peat Bid Is On

x_posts.label: Post 5
x_posts.text:
Teagan Kavan. Junior. Katie Stewart. Junior. Both come back for 2027.

Texas softball isn't finished building its dynasty. The three-peat conversation starts today. 🤠

#TexasSoftball #HookEm
